reasoning: While LLM fidelity scores indicate excellent text extraction quality (composite 0.9973), programmatic detectors identified three major structural and accessibility issues. These include a duplicate full-page image alongside extracted text, three fallback-rendered images, and truncated alt text for two images. Based on the count-based routing thresholds, the presence of 3 major programmatic defects requires a HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 3 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=3. Examples: p1: "The header of an official letter from the North Ca"; p1: "The bottom portion of an official document featuri"; p1: "The bottom portion of an official letter from the ". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ment of Environmental Quality, featuring the state"; p1: "...ina Department of Environmental Quality, featuring"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.
The source PDF contains an underlined sentence for emphasis in the fourth paragraph. The output JSON fails to preserve this underline.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference.
concerns.output_quote: Please attach this letter to the cover of your existing mining permit for future reference.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Remove the redundant full-page image to prevent duplicate screen reader readings
Fix the truncated alt text for the extracted images to ensure WCAG AA compliance
Verify the fallback images and replace or remove them if they do not add meaningful content
Strip extraneous header and footer text from the body
